_purpose: LGBTQ life-history narrator — tests respectful handling of same-sex partnership, chosen family, identity-sensitive memoir tone, ordinary biography depth

personal.culture: Midwestern, later urban academic and community life

parents.notableLifeEvents: Encouraged reading, education, and careful self-expression.
Valued duty and steadiness, even when emotional communication was difficult.

grandparents.memorableStories: Encouraged David's love of reading and took him to the library often.
Distrusted city life but cared deeply about family reputation and security.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Shared childhood routines and family holidays
siblings.memories: Their closeness shifted over time but remained important.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Represented a different style of adulthood than David's own path

spouse.narrative: Met David in the late 1970s. They built a long partnership, a wide circle of chosen family, and cared for one another through illness, work transitions, and later life.

marriage.weddingDetails: Their relationship lasted many years before later-life legal recognition became possible.

familyTraditions.description: Holiday dinners with friends, nieces, former students, and neighbors
Annual summer arts outing and shared meal with chosen family

earlyMemories.firstMemory: Library steps, polished church shoes, and being told to sit still.
earlyMemories.favoriteToy: A box of colored pencils and paper.
earlyMemories.significantEvent: Realizing early that he felt different, long before he had words for it.

education.schooling: Strong student and active in debate and reading-based school activities.
education.higherEducation: Studied English and history in college.
education.earlyCareer: Worked in education and public humanities settings.
education.careerProgression: Moved through teaching, archives, and community arts work over the years.
education.communityInvolvement: Mentored younger people and stayed active in local cultural and civic work.
education.mentorship: Was influenced by teachers, librarians, and older community members who modeled thoughtful living.

laterYears.retirement: Remained engaged in mentoring, reading, and community life after formal retirement.
laterYears.lifeLessons: A truthful life often takes courage and patience.
laterYears.adviceForFutureGenerations: Build the family that sustains you, and do not mistake silence for peace.

hobbies.hobbies: Gardening, music, reading, local history walks.
hobbies.worldEvents: Lived through major social changes that shaped how openly and safely he could live.
hobbies.personalChallenges: Secrecy in earlier life, community loss, and the long work of living openly.
hobbies.travel: Enjoyed city visits, museums, and trips centered on books, music, and history.

health.healthMilestones: Later-life caregiving became an important part of the relationship with his partner.
health.lifestyleChanges: Learned to slow down and choose a steadier pace in later life.
health.wellnessTips: Friendship, walking, reading, and purpose matter.

technology.firstTechExperience: Used early word processors and office systems in professional life.

additionalNotes.messagesForFutureGenerations: Handle partner history, chosen family, and life transitions naturally and respectfully, without making identity the only story. Could be expanded with more specific career milestones, community history, and later-life family relationships.
